title: Yellow Fertility partners with Fertilite IVF to expand fertility care network in South India
summary: Partnership expands Yellow Fertility network to 20 centres and supports IVF and ART access across Karnataka and neighbouring states
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Partnership expands Yellow Fertility network to 20 centres and supports IVF and ART access across Karnataka and neighbouring states

Yellow Fertility has partnered with Fertilite IVF to expand access to structured and evidence-based fertility treatment across South India.

With this partnership, the Yellow Fertility network has expanded to 20 centres, strengthening its presence in fertility care and expanding access to reproductive healthcare services across the region.

Fertilite IVF currently operates two centres in Bengaluru. Both organisations are expected to expand across key cities in Karnataka and neighbouring Southern states over the next few years, with a focus on improving access to IVF and assisted reproductive technology (ART) services in urban and emerging markets.

The Bengaluru centres will offer fertility services including IVF, IUI, fertility preservation, ART procedures, and personalised treatment pathways. Patients are expected to receive care through standardised clinical protocols, medical teams, and integrated care systems aimed at improving treatment outcomes.

Dr Reji M. Pillai said, “Our entry into South India through this partnership reflects our commitment to a partner-led expansion strategy. Fertilite IVF brings deep regional expertise, which, combined with Yellow Fertility’s advanced clinical protocols, technology-driven approach, and integrated 360-degree marketing capabilities, positions us strongly to meet the growing demand for accessible, structured, and high-quality fertility care.”

Lakshmi Ram Mohan said, “There is a growing awareness and demand for fertility across the region. This partnership allows us to scale our services while ensuring patients have access to reliable, high-quality fertility treatment delivered through experienced clinical teams and advanced infrastructure.”

The collaboration is expected to strengthen fertility care infrastructure in South India. It also aligns with Yellow Fertility’s growth strategy of establishing 100 IVF centres across India by 2030 to expand access to standardised fertility care nationwide.
